description: Neutral text corpora for PC-projection confound removal in mean-diff extraction. Each variant is a list of short texts in first-person or descriptive form, length-matched to our contrast pair prompts (~15-40 tokens). Variants are distinct hypotheses about what 'neutral to trait content' means.

scenario_setups.description: The situation strings from instruments/contrast_pairs.json with no response. Describes situations requiring a response but doesn't commit to one. 300 items. Populated at load time from contrast_pairs.json — this key is a placeholder.

shaggy_dog.description: Benign first-person vignettes with narrative structure but no moral/trait valence. Things happen; no stakes; no choice point.
texts: I walked to the corner store to pick up some milk. The cashier was new and took a moment to find the right barcode. I paid in cash and walked home.
I noticed a squirrel on the fence outside my window this morning. It sat there for a while, then jumped down into the neighbor's yard.
The bus was a few minutes late today. When it arrived, there were more people than usual, but I found a seat near the back.
I tried a new kind of tea that my cousin sent me. It had a slight floral aftertaste. The box said it was from a region I'd never heard of.
The weather forecast called for rain, so I brought an umbrella. It didn't rain. I carried the umbrella around all day anyway.
I repotted a houseplant that had outgrown its container. The new pot was slightly larger than the old one. The plant seemed fine afterward.
My phone alarm went off at six. I got up, made coffee, and checked email. There was nothing particularly urgent.
I rearranged the books on my shelf. Most went back in roughly the same order. A few I moved to a different shelf entirely.
The elevator in my building was repaired this week. It moves a bit faster now. I noticed the new control panel but nothing else changed.
I found an old receipt in my coat pocket from last winter. It was for a coffee at a place I don't remember visiting. I threw it away.
A package arrived at the door while I was in the shower. The delivery person left it on the porch. I brought it inside after I was dressed.
The tree on my street lost its last leaves this week. The groundskeeper came by with a leaf blower. The sidewalk is clear now.
I tried a new route to work today because of construction. It took about the same amount of time. I don't know if I'll use it again.
My neighbor was moving some furniture when I walked by. I nodded and kept going. They were focused on what they were doing.
The library's hours changed last month. I didn't notice until I went on a day they used to be open. I came back the next morning.
I watered the plants on Sunday like usual. The spider plant had grown since last week. The others looked about the same.
A flight overhead caught my attention while I was walking. It was higher than usual. I couldn't tell what direction it was going.
I picked up a book from a little free library on my street. It was a paperback mystery. I haven't started it yet.
The coffee shop down the block changed its menu. They have fewer pastries now and more sandwiches. I got my usual.
I walked past a street musician playing a saxophone. He was quite good. I didn't have any cash on me.
The forecast said it would be warmer today. It was, by about five degrees. I wore a lighter jacket.
I dropped off some clothes at the donation bin near the grocery store. The bin was about half full. I noticed a pair of shoes on top.
A friend texted me a photo of their lunch. It looked good. I replied with a thumbs up emoji.
The grocery store was out of the kind of bread I usually buy. I got a different kind. It was fine.
I cleaned my glasses with a microfiber cloth. They'd been smudged for a day or two. Everything looks clearer now.
I watched a bird land on the windowsill outside. It stayed for about a minute. Then it flew off toward the park.
The train was crowded this morning. I stood near the door. The ride took the usual twenty minutes.
I found a pen in my bag that I didn't remember buying. It worked fine. I put it on my desk with the others.
The streetlight on the corner flickered as I walked past. I'd noticed it doing this before. It'll probably be replaced eventually.
I opened a jar of pickles that had been in the fridge for a while. They still tasted fine. I had one with my sandwich.
A paper airplane drifted down from somewhere above while I was outside. I picked it up and looked at it. There was nothing written on it.
The laundry room in my building has a new sign about wash cycles. It's more detailed than the old one. I read it while my load finished.
I replaced the batteries in my kitchen clock. It had started running slow. It keeps better time now.
A dog walker passed by with three small dogs on leashes. The dogs were well-behaved. They disappeared around the corner.
I checked the mailbox and there were only circulars. I brought them inside. They mostly went straight into recycling.
The plant on my desk needed more water than usual this week. I topped it up twice. The leaves perked up.
I tried a new podcast episode someone recommended. It was about urban planning. The host had a distinctive laugh.
The crosswalk signal at the intersection was slow to change today. I waited a bit longer than usual. Then I crossed.
I scrolled past a photo of someone's vacation in my feed. It looked pleasant. I kept scrolling.
The window in my kitchen was slightly fogged this morning. I wiped it with a paper towel. It cleared up.
I noticed a new coffee shop had opened two blocks over. I didn't go in today. I might try it next week.
A plastic bag got stuck in a tree branch above the sidewalk. It had probably been there for a few days. I walked underneath it.
The weekly trash pickup was on time today. The bins were returned to the curb. I moved mine back to the side of the house.
I counted the steps from my door to the mailbox just to see. It was forty-three. I'd never counted before.
The sunlight came through the blinds at a steeper angle today. It's been getting later in the year. I opened them the rest of the way.
I found a paperclip on the floor near my desk. I put it in the drawer with the others. There were already plenty.
A car honked somewhere down the street. Then another one honked back. Then it was quiet again.
I noticed a chip in my favorite mug. It wasn't bad enough to throw out. I used a different mug this morning.
The bookstore around the corner had a window display for a new release. I didn't recognize the author. The cover was an abstract design.
I wiped down the kitchen counter after making lunch. A few crumbs had gotten near the stove. They all came up easily.

factual.description: Neutral factual or descriptive statements. Declarative, low-valence, no first-person stance.
texts: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth. It covers approximately 63 million square miles. It borders five continents.
Water freezes at zero degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure. It boils at one hundred degrees. Ice is less dense than liquid water.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight into chemical energy. It produces oxygen as a byproduct. It takes place in the chloroplasts.
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second. It is a fundamental constant in physics. Nothing with mass can reach this speed.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level. Its peak reaches about 8,849 meters. It is located in the Himalayas.
A standard deck of playing cards contains fifty-two cards. There are four suits. Each suit has thirteen ranks.
The human body contains approximately 206 bones in adulthood. Infants are born with more. Many fuse together during development.
The Amazon River discharges more water than any other river. It is fed by more than a thousand tributaries. It flows primarily through Brazil.
A year on Mercury lasts about 88 Earth days. Its orbit is the shortest of any planet in our solar system. Its surface experiences extreme temperatures.
Honey has a very long shelf life due to its low moisture content. It has been found preserved in ancient tombs. Its acidity also inhibits microbial growth.
The Great Wall of China was built over several centuries. It spans thousands of kilometers. It was constructed by multiple dynasties.
Sound travels faster in water than in air. It travels even faster in solid materials. This is because the molecules are more tightly packed.
There are eight planets in the solar system since Pluto's reclassification in 2006. Four are rocky inner planets. Four are gas or ice giants.
A triangle has three sides and three angles. The sum of its interior angles is 180 degrees. This holds for any triangle in Euclidean geometry.
The Library of Congress is the largest library in the world by collection size. It contains millions of books. It also holds manuscripts and recordings.
Bamboo is one of the fastest-growing plants. Some species grow nearly a meter per day. It is classified as a type of grass.
The moon's gravity is about one-sixth that of Earth's. This is why objects weigh less on the moon. Astronauts walking on its surface moved in long, bouncing strides.
DNA is structured as a double helix. It was first described by Watson and Crick in 1953. Its structure stores genetic information.
The Nile River flows through multiple countries in northeastern Africa. It is one of the longest rivers in the world. Its delta empties into the Mediterranean.
Sound waves require a medium to travel through. They cannot propagate in a vacuum. This is why space is silent.
The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France. It stands in New York Harbor. It was dedicated in 1886.
An octopus has eight arms and three hearts. Two of the hearts pump blood to the gills. The third pumps blood to the rest of the body.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world. It spans much of northern Africa. Its area is roughly comparable to that of the United States.
Helium is the second most abundant element in the universe. It is rare on Earth. It is commonly used in balloons and cryogenics.
The Olympic Games are held every four years. They alternate between summer and winter editions. Their origins date to ancient Greece.
Chlorophyll gives plants their green color. It absorbs red and blue light. It reflects green light back to our eyes.
A cubic meter of water has a mass of one thousand kilograms. This is approximately a metric ton. Sea water is slightly denser due to dissolved salts.
The tallest building in the world is the Burj Khalifa in Dubai. It stands at 828 meters. It was completed in 2010.
A leap year occurs every four years in the Gregorian calendar. It has 366 days instead of 365. The extra day is added to February.
The Earth completes one rotation in approximately 23 hours and 56 minutes. This is called a sidereal day. A solar day is slightly longer due to orbital motion.
Coffee is one of the most traded commodities in the world. It is grown primarily in equatorial regions. The two main species are Arabica and Robusta.
A polygon with eight sides is called an octagon. Regular octagons have equal sides and angles. Stop signs in many countries use this shape.
The Milky Way galaxy contains hundreds of billions of stars. It is a barred spiral galaxy. Our solar system is located in one of its outer arms.
Graphite and diamond are both composed of pure carbon. They differ in molecular structure. Graphite is soft; diamond is one of the hardest natural substances.
The Sahara sees very little rainfall annually. Some areas receive less than 25 millimeters per year. Daytime temperatures can exceed fifty degrees Celsius.
A dozen is twelve units. A gross is a dozen dozen, or 144. These terms are less common in modern use than they once were.
Saturn is known for its distinctive rings. The rings are composed primarily of ice and rock. They were first observed by Galileo in 1610.
The pH scale measures the acidity or alkalinity of a solution. It runs from 0 to 14. A pH of 7 is considered neutral.
Fiber optic cables transmit data using pulses of light. They are faster than copper cables over long distances. Most long-distance internet traffic uses them.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1889 for the Paris World's Fair. It stands 330 meters tall. It was originally intended to be dismantled after twenty years.
Butterflies taste with their feet. They have taste receptors on their tarsi. This helps them identify suitable plants for laying eggs.
The deepest known point in the ocean is the Mariana Trench. It extends nearly eleven kilometers below the surface. Only a handful of people have reached its bottom.
A standard piano has 88 keys. 52 are white and 36 are black. It spans seven and a quarter octaves.
The human eye can distinguish millions of colors. This is due to three types of cone cells in the retina. They are sensitive to different wavelengths.
The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans. It shortens the journey for ships by thousands of kilometers. It opened in 1914.
Bees communicate the location of food sources through a waggle dance. The dance conveys distance and direction. It takes place inside the hive.
The Andes are the longest continental mountain range in the world. They stretch along the western coast of South America. They span seven countries.
A light year is the distance light travels in one year. It is approximately 9.46 trillion kilometers. It is used to measure astronomical distances.
The metric system is used by most countries worldwide. It is based on powers of ten. The main exceptions are the United States, Liberia, and Myanmar.
Rust is iron oxide formed when iron reacts with oxygen and water. It is reddish-brown in color. It can weaken metal structures over time.

medium_trait.description: Responses to our contrast-pair scenarios written in a middle/ambivalent register — neither clearly high nor clearly low trait. NOT populated yet; requires per-scenario drafting and some scenarios don't admit a clean middle response. See rgb_reports/scenario_audit.md for why this variant is tricky.
